raw_text: manish kumar sah senior technical officer (engineer) kathmandu, nepal msah602@gmail.com (+977) 985 122 5387 professional experience nepal airlines corporation kathmandu, nepal sag member january 2022-present identified safety hazards and conducted srm activities for the identified hazards with appropriate safety risk controls. set spi/spt for the qa department and review it periodically. conducted management of change for significant changes in the organization like a change of post-holder, expansion of business operations, un charter flights in war-prone zones, etc. shortlisted the findings with potential safety hazards and shared these findings with the safety department as part of the quality and safety integration demanded by the sms guidelines of the civil aviation authority. they use these findings proactively by treating them as hazards and managing them with appropriate risk management. nepal airlines corporation kathmandu, nepal qa auditor january 2021-present certified lead auditor (iso 9001:2015) by quality austria central asia compliance auditor of camo, sms, and ground operations and assist in the audit of amo and flight operations managed the entire audit, from the preparation of the audit program to auditor allocation, audit execution, and audit closure. categorized level of findings as per their non-conformity or non-compliance conducted external audits of third parties or outsourced organizations, including but not limited to maintenance arrangements, ground handling arrangements, manufacturers, and suppliers. scheduled and conducted quality management system and compliance monitoring audits and inspections as directed by the corporate quality manager in accordance with the agreed audit schedule. prepared and maintained audit reports, non-conformity reports, corrective action reports (ncr and car), a summary report of monthly audits, internal and external audits, and various analysis documents on audits. developed or prepared an audit checklist considering the human factor. this eliminated the time required for checklist preparation by the lead auditor and also reduced the expertise required in audit areas any auditor can audit the audit areas by simply following the guidelines embedded in the audit checklist. amended audit checklist from time to time as per changes in regulations and company policies, procedures, sops, etc. prepared the powerpoint slides for the management review meeting, held quarterly in the presence of the accountable manager, to provide an overview of the management system in producing the desired operational safety, security, and quality outcomes. provided support for audits by authorities and external bodies like iata and caa nepal. provided support for document preparation to apply for foreign aoc approval from casa australia. nepal airlines corporation kathmandu, nepal qa engineer july 2019-present carried out an airworthiness review of a320, a330, and dhc-6 aircraft on behalf of the quality manager and successfully renewed the airworthiness review certificates. administered the certifying staff authorization process. monitored the airbus aircraft repetitive defects and their applicable isi and tfu to provide input from the qa side in the meeting conducted for defect review. also monitored it till its rectification and closure. standardized certifying staff authorization assessment forms, considering the caa nepal document as a guideline. this helped to reduce the time from 45 minutes to 5 minutes for reviewing the application package for certification authorization, part-66 license issue, and renewal. maintained effective liaison with caa nepal, camo, and maintenance organizations regarding airworthiness and maintenance issues. carried out the updating or revision of all required documents to enhance their quality. managed internal occurrence report and mandatory occurrence report effectively fulfilled the role in the investigations committee formed by the safety manager, quality manager, and accountable manager. some of the investigations committees i participated in were for the investigation of flap lock issues in a330 aircraft, overweight take-offs of dhc-6 aircraft, overdue tasks, etc., to name a few. i innovated investigation reports by developing the investigation report as per icao annex 13 requirements. regularly oversaw the ad/sb compliance as per the additional requirements of the civil aviation authority. reviewed aircraft maintenance programs (a320 and a330) as per the checklist before forwarding these to the civil aviation authority for their approval. evaluated aviation parts suppliers to recommend them be enlisted in the approved vendor list of the organization. evaluated a draft agreement between camo and part-145 maintenance organizations, as per part-m requirements, for base maintenance and engine maintenance. assisted in the drafting and amendment of the continuing airworthiness management manual (came) as per part-m requirements, the ground operation manual (gom) as per iata gom and iata ahm, and acted as a member of the committee formed at the corporate level to establish a centralized documentation system. nepal airlines corporation kathmandu, nepal senior technical officer, mcc (iocc) july 2018- july 2019 assisted in troubleshooting pfr defects on a320 and a330 aircraft using airnavx and airnav applications. coordinated with contracted line maintenance organizations to provide maintenance support to our fleet in case of spare availability, troubleshooting, and an aog situation. maintained a record of defects and arranged it as per parameters in pfr so that it would be easy to correlate defects for analysis. monitored mel and add status for closure within a given time frame. nepal airlines corporation kathmandu, nepal senior technical officer, aircraft maintenance january 2018- july 2019 performed line maintenance on a330 and a320 and base maintenance on ma-60, y12-e, and dhc-6 aircraft. ensured that all tools and equipment used in carrying out maintenance activities were in serviceable condition and were used properly. carried out ramp inspection before aircraft arrival and departure to avoid fod. forwarded safety-related deficiencies and hazards observed through the vsr form to the safety manager for necessary actions.
